title: task sync --watch (explicit long-running sync loop)
acceptance_criteria: task sync without --watch is a one-shot pull/push and exits (default behavior already from TTR-E07-S02).

task sync --watch is an explicit long-running command that loops every config.json:git.auto_sync_seconds (or the --interval override) until ctx is cancelled (Ctrl+C). Exits 0 on clean shutdown, 130 on SIGINT.
Rejected as ErrUsage if config.json:git.auto_sync_seconds is null AND --interval is not given.
Tested via context cancellation: the loop exits cleanly within one tick of cancellation.
Avoids the conceptual flaw of trying to run a background goroutine inside a one-shot CLI invocation that would otherwise exit immediately.
